topTargets.name: Denzel Boston
topTargets.reasoning: Primary 1.13 target: Consensus #9 WR5 Tier 2 (CLE landing spot) and ADP 16.11 — sits right at my pick by market price, exactly the kind of consensus-confirmed WR a low-RSP-affinity GM takes. Roster has 6 WRs vs 8 RBs, so WR is the clean need, and Boston's Consensus/ADP alignment beats Waldman's lukewarm RSP #25 grade for my weighting (I weight ADP 4x heavier than RSP).

topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Backup at 1.13 if Boston gone: Consensus #12 WR6 Tier 2 (NYJ) with ADP 17.20 — both boards anchor him as a late-1st value. 50% Consensus + 40% ADP both put him squarely in my range; RSP DoT 83.9 with the Deebo comp doesn't move me much given low affinity, but it's not a red flag either.

topTargets.name: Jadarian Price
topTargets.reasoning: Available-at-1.13 fallback: Consensus #7 RB2 Tier 2 (SEA) with ADP 16.05 — strongest ADP-confirmed name likely sliding to me. Already at 8 RBs so it's a luxury, but if both Boston and Cooper Jr. are gone the consensus/market gap is too big to pass; I'd rather take the BPA per ADP than reach down a full tier for a WR.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: 2.13 target (~pick 29): ADP 29.06 lands exactly at my slot, Consensus #24 WR14 Tier 4 (WAS). Market-priced perfectly for a low-RSP GM — I don't need Waldman's RSP #18 Jayden Reed comp to take a player both Consensus and ADP agree on at this spot.

topTargets.name: Chris Bell
topTargets.reasoning: 2.13 alt: Consensus #19 WR10 Tier 2 (MIA) with ADP 28.24 — a Tier 2 falling to early 2nd is exactly the Consensus-anchored value play my weighting rewards. AJ Brown comp upside per RSP is gravy, not the reason; I'm taking the higher Consensus tier here over Williams if Bell is on the board.

capPosture: Tight at $41.9M of $45M used with 23 active and 8 contracts expiring; rookies must go taxi (50% hit) to fit, which works since taxi is empty.

wildcard.name: Ty Simpson
wildcard.reasoning: 3.13 dart throw: Consensus #37 QB2 (LAR backup) with ADP 23.81 — ADP loves him way more than Consensus, and as a low-RSP/high-ADP GM I'd chase the market on a backup QB lottery ticket in round 3 with 8 contracts expiring next year and only 3 QBs rostered.

summary: Da Dangsters enter 1.13 cap-strapped but with empty taxi slots, so the plan is to take ADP-confirmed talent that fits the 50/40/10 board lean and stash on taxi. WR is the positional priority (6 WRs vs 8 RBs) and the 1.13 board sets up beautifully — Boston, Cooper Jr., and Price all carry ADPs of 16-17, meaning at least one falls to me. Round 2 and 3 follow the same script: take the highest Consensus tier where ADP confirms, ignore Waldman-only sleepers like Skyler Bell or Myles Montgomery.
